title: JSON Patch and JSON Merge Patch
description: Schema describing JSON Patch (RFC 6902) operations for modifying JSON documents, and JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396) for merging partial updates into JSON documents.

jsonPatch.title: JSON Patch (RFC 6902)
jsonPatch.description: A JSON Patch document is a JSON document that represents an array of operations to apply to a target JSON document.

path.description: A JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) string identifying the target location within the document.

value.description: The value to use for add, replace, or test operations.

from.description: A JSON Pointer string identifying the source location for move or copy operations.

jsonMergePatch.title: JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396)
jsonMergePatch.description: A JSON Merge Patch document describes changes to a target JSON document using a syntax that closely mirrors the document being modified. Recipients process merge patches by comparing the content with the current target document: members present in the patch replace the corresponding target values, null values remove the corresponding target members, and members not present in the patch remain unchanged.

oneOf.description: An object whose members represent changes to apply. A null value removes the member; an object value is applied recursively; any other value replaces the target member.
When the merge patch is not an object, the entire target is replaced.
When the merge patch is not an object, the entire target is replaced.
When the merge patch is not an object, the entire target is replaced.
When the merge patch is not an object, the entire target is replaced.
A null merge patch replaces the entire target with null.
